hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity at position 11 and introducing aromatic residues at position 5 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by better engaging SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions while reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective at generating high-affinity binders but insufficient for achieving the required level of SSTR2 selectivity. Targeted mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to disrupt conserved off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target binding in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).
Introducing mutations at these positions with residues that disrupt off-target interactions while preserving native-like backbone geometry and pharmacophore integrity.
